questions.question: The maximum number of Buddhist stupas had been built by which of the following rulers?
questions.solution:
Ashoka and Buddhist Stupas

Tradition holds that Emperor Ashoka was responsible for building 84,000 stupas across his empire to enshrine the relics of the Buddha. While the number may be hyperbolic, he was undoubtedly the most prolific builder of these structures, including the original foundations of the Sanchi Stupa.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct?
questions.option_1: LAN supports networking over large geographic areas.
questions.option_2: ASCII represents characters as numerical codes.
questions.option_3: ASCII is primarily used for high-speed data transmission networks.
questions.option_4: CD is a volatile storage medium.
questions.solution:
ASCII Character Encoding

ASCII (American Standard Code for Information Interchange) is a character encoding standard that represents text in computers and telecommunications. It assigns a numerical code (0 to 127) to each character, such as letters, digits, and punctuation marks.

questions.question: Which dynasty replaced the Shungas in Magadha?
questions.solution:
The Kanva Dynasty

The Shunga dynasty was overthrown in approximately 73 BCE by Vasudeva Kanva, who founded the Kanva dynasty. The Kanvas ruled Magadha for a relatively short period before being displaced by the Satavahanas from the south.

questions.question: Which of the following caves contain rock cut paintings dating back to the stone age era?
questions.solution:
Bhimbetka Rock Shelters

The Bhimbetka caves in Madhya Pradesh contain some of the oldest rock paintings in India, dating back to the Paleolithic and Mesolithic eras (Stone Age). They depict scenes of hunting, dancing, and daily life of early humans.

questions.question: Who among the following was considered the first notable king of the Pushyabhuti dynasty, ruling from 580-605 CE?
questions.solution:
Prabhakaravardhana

Prabhakaravardhana is considered the first significant king of the Pushyabhuti (or Vardhana) dynasty. He was the father of Harshavardhana and assumed the title of Maharajadhiraja, indicating his successful military campaigns against the Hunas and Gurjaras.

questions.question: Who succeeded Dhruvasena?
questions.solution:
Maitraka Succession

In the Maitraka dynasty of Vallabhi, Dharapatta succeeded his brother Dhruvasena I. The Maitraka rulers were known for their patronage of learning and the university at Vallabhi.

questions.question: Chandragupta Maurya is said to have converted to:
questions.solution:
Chandragupta Maurya's Conversion

According to Jain tradition, Chandragupta Maurya abdicated his throne in his later years and converted to Jainism. He became a disciple of the monk Bhadrabahu and spent his final days practicing Sallekhana (fasting unto death) at Shravanabelagola in Karnataka.

questions.question: Which of the following caves are famous for its high citadel?
questions.solution:
Junagadh Caves

The Junagadh Buddhist Caves (specifically the Uparkot caves) are famous for their unique rock-cut architecture that mimics a high citadel or multi-story structure. They were carved into the sandstone during the 2nd-3rd centuries CE.

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the Ettuttokai (Eight Anthologies) of the Sangam literature?
questions.solution:
Sangam Literature: Tolkappiyam

Tolkappiyam is NOT one of the Eight Anthologies (Ettuttokai); rather, it is the oldest surviving work of Tamil grammar and poetics. The Ettuttokai consists of eight specific poetic collections like Narrinai and Purananuru.

questions.question: Which of the following was the famous ruler of the Satavahana dynasty?
questions.solution:
Gautamiputra Satakarni

Gautamiputra Satakarni was the greatest ruler of the Satavahana dynasty. He is celebrated for restoring the prestige of his family by defeating the Shakas (Western Kshatrapas) and ending the line of Nahapana.

questions.question: The Mauryan period saw the development of which script for inscriptions?
questions.solution:
Brahmi Script

The Brahmi script was the primary script used for imperial inscriptions during the Mauryan period. It is the ancestor of most modern Indian scripts. While the language was often Prakrit, Brahmi served as the written medium for Ashoka's edicts throughout most of India.

questions.question: The famous 'Trimurti' sculpture is present in which of the following caves?
questions.solution:
Trimurti of Elephanta

The Elephanta Caves, located on Gharapuri Island near Mumbai, house the magnificent 20-foot high Trimurti (Sadasiva) sculpture. It represents three aspects of Lord Shiva: the Creator, the Preserver, and the Destroyer.

questions.question: The 'Reclining Statue of Lord Vishnu' is found in which of the following caves?
questions.solution:
Undavalli Caves

The Undavalli Caves in Andhra Pradesh are famous for a massive monolithic statue of Lord Vishnu in a reclining posture (Anantashayana Vishnu), carved out of a single block of sandstone in the 4th-5th century CE.

questions.question: The most important source for Samudragupta's reign is:
questions.solution:
Prayagraj Prashasti

The Prayagraj Prashasti (Allahabad Pillar Inscription), composed by Harishena, is the most authentic source for Samudragupta's reign. It provides a detailed chronological list of his conquests and his poetic and musical talents.

questions.question: Which powerful ruler of the Pallavas constructed the Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram?
questions.solution:
The Shore Temple

The Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram was built by the Pallava King Narasimhavarman II (also known as Rajasimha). It is one of the oldest structural (not rock-cut) stone temples in South India and is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

questions.question: An internal trade route during the post-Mauryan empires, Uttarapatha, connected the:
questions.option_1: western and southern parts of India
questions.option_2: northern and southern parts of India with the northwestern region
questions.option_3: southern and eastern parts of India
questions.option_4: northern and eastern parts of India with the northwestern region
questions.solution:
Uttarapatha Trade Route

The Uttarapatha (Northern Path) was a major transcontinental trade route that connected the northern and eastern parts of India (like Pataliputra and Tamralipti) with the northwestern regions (Taxila and Central Asia).

questions.question: The Shakas were defeated by which tribal group before entering India?
questions.solution:
The Yueh-chi Pressure

The Shakas (Scythians) were pushed out of their original homelands in Central Asia by the Yueh-chi (who later became the Kushanas). This displacement forced the Shakas to migrate southward into the Indian subcontinent.

questions.question: The Scythians, or Shakas, originally came from _______.
questions.solution:
Origin of the Shakas

The Scythians, known in Indian sources as the Shakas, originally came from Central Asia. They were nomadic tribes who eventually established several 'Kshatrapas' (provinces) across North and Western India.

questions.question: Which ruler of the Western Kshatrapas dynasty is primarily known for restoring the Sudarshana Lake in Kathiawar?
questions.solution:
Rudradaman I and Sudarshana Lake

Rudradaman I of the Western Kshatrapas is famous for the Junagadh Rock Inscription. It records that he used his own private funds to repair the Sudarshana Lake dam after it was destroyed by a storm, showing his concern for public welfare.

questions.question: Rudradaman, who ruled in the middle of second century AD territories of Mathura and Gujarat belonged to which dynasty?
questions.solution:
Rudradaman's Dynasty

Rudradaman I belonged to the Shaka (Western Kshatrapa) dynasty. He was a great conqueror and a patron of Sanskrit; his Junagadh inscription is considered one of the earliest major inscriptions written in pure Sanskrit.
